Why Multi-Cap funds could be the smartest diversification bet right now? All you need to know about them

Bitcoin at $59,900 amid inflation concerns, here's what experts think lies ahead
Bitcoin price tumbles below $60,000 to 20-month low as tech rout, rate fears rattle crypto market: What lies ahead?

articles.title: Multibagger HDFC AMC to announce June quarter results on this date. Check details
articles.summary: HDFC AMC's board will meet on July 15, 2026, to review financial results for June 2026. The trading window for shares closes from July 1-17, 2026. The firm launched a new sustainable investment strategy, HDFC Growth for GOOD Portfolio, aiming for positive societal outcomes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Chart Beat: Q1FY27 growth seen steady for consumer staples—but it’s still a pricing story.
articles.summary: Nomura analysts expect aggregate revenue growth of 10.4% year-on-year for consumer staples companies under their coverage in Q1FY27.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why Multi-Cap funds could be the smartest diversification bet right now? All you need to know about them
articles.summary: Multi Cap Funds provide a single scheme for investors to access large-, mid-, and small-cap stocks while adhering to a mandatory allocation of 25% each across these segments, ensuring genuine diversification and balanced performance during market fluctuations.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Multibagger small cap stock Standard Engineering share price to be in focus on Monday; here's why
articles.summary: SETL's acquisition of a controlling stake in GScale Energy marks its entry into the AI data centre market. With a strategic investment of ₹500 crore, SETL seeks to leverage GScale's expertise for growth, targeting 40-50% increase in core revenue by FY2027.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: IPO GMPs: CSM Technologies IPO vs Knack Packaging IPO vs Aastha Spintex IPO; here's what GMP hints
articles.summary: India's IPO market remains vibrant with multiple offerings like Knack Packaging and Aastha Spintex. The subscription for CSM Technologies is progressing well, with strong grey market premiums suggesting positive listing outcomes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Foreign investors exit India funds as AI boom redirects capital. Will inflows return?
articles.summary: Foreign investors pulled money from India-focused funds in the March quarter as capital shifted toward AI-linked markets such as Korea and Taiwan, even as some investors see early signs of macro stabilization in India.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank remain top bets as valuations stay attractive, says Kotak
articles.summary: Kotak Institutional Equities supports leading private banks like HDFC and ICICI, believing they will benefit from improved loan yields and lower funding costs. They remain cautious on loan demand but expect strong asset quality and less pressure on corporate balance sheets.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy: Anand Rathi bullish on Hindustan Copper shares, sees 45% upside in the PSU stock; here’s why
articles.summary: Hindustan Copper share price has fallen 10% in one month, and has dropped 6% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis. The PSU stock has gained 12% in six months, and has rallied 90% in one year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Tata Motors PV is on a clear road. JLR remains the speed bump.
articles.summary: The carmaker has laid out an ambitious domestic growth roadmap, but investors remain focused on Jaguar Land Rover's recovery as the key driver of future stock performance.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Bitcoin at $59,900 amid inflation concerns, here's what experts think lies ahead
articles.summary: Bitcoin price today: The world's largest cryptocurrency fell to $59,934.04, down 2.66%, while the overall crypto market cap fell to $2.06 trillion amid declining investor sentiment and hawkish inflation fears.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Bitcoin price tumbles below $60,000 to 20-month low as tech rout, rate fears rattle crypto market: What lies ahead?
articles.summary: With Bitcoin's recent drop below $60,000 amidst a tech stock sell-off, analysts express concerns about ongoing volatility driven by macroeconomic factors. Despite slowing outflows from ETFs, the crypto market remains weak.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Upcoming IPO: Sathya Agencies, Kanohar Electricals, Torrent Gas get SEBI nod to launch IPOs
articles.summary: Sathya Agencies, Kanohar Electricals, and Torrent Gas received SEBI approval for their IPOs. Sathya plans to raise ₹600 crore, while Kanohar aims for ₹300 crore, focusing on expansion. Torrent's IPO follows a confidential filing, allowing for regulatory review without public disclosure.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: 1600% returns in one year! Multibagger stock to watch on Monday after this development
articles.summary: Magnus Steel & Infra share price has fallen 46% in one month, but has risen 13% in three months. The smallcap stock has delivered multibagger returns of 154% in six months, and has jumped by a staggering 1,606% in one year.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: OpenAI IPO: ChatGPT maker considers delaying public issue to 2027, stresses on $1 trillion valuation, says report
articles.summary: OpenAI’s advisers presented company executives with the option of waiting until 2027 to go public with a $1 trillion valuation, or lower the targeted valuation for a quicker listing, NYT report said.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Turtlemint Fintech IPO listing date on 29 June: Here’s what GMP hints ahead of debut
articles.summary: Turtlemint Fintech IPO listing is scheduled on 29 June, after a subscription period from 19 to 23 June. The IPO received strong demand, particularly from retail investors. The share price is fixed between ₹144-152, with a total offering value of up to ₹660.72 crore.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Is ITC stock nearing the end of a 21-month correction? Bajaj Broking sees a 14% rally in 6 months
articles.summary: Bajaj Broking anticipates ITC stock to rise towards ₹330, supported by a favorable risk-reward setup at a key support level. The stock has shown positive momentum, suggesting an investment opportunity in the next six months with a projected return of 14%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: ‘Gold is about to rise for a long time,’ predicts Rich Dad Poor Dad author Robert Kiyosaki
articles.summary: Robert Kiyosaki maintains a bullish outlook on gold, predicting a prolonged rally amid rising global debt and deteriorating economic conditions. He urges investors to consider physical assets and emphasizes the significance of gold and silver as 'real money' during economic uncertainty.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO allotment next week. GMP, steps to check share allotment status online
articles.summary: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO allotment date which is likely 29 June 2026. Investors can check Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO allotment status online through the websites of BSE, NSE and IPO registrar. MUFG Intime India is the Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO registrar.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold rate today slips below $4,000 for the first time since 2025, silver price also declines. Is a bigger crash coming?
articles.summary: Gold and silver face continued pressure amid macroeconomic challenges. Analysts note gold dropped below $4,000, with bearish trends potentially pushing it further down. Despite near-term volatility, experts maintain a positive long-term outlook.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Rajesh Palviya of Axis Sec suggests JSW Infrastructure, Ind-Swift Laboratories, Welspun Living shares to buy next week
articles.summary: The Indian stock market will remain closed on June 26 for the Muharram holiday. Benchmark indices extended their winning streak ahead of the holiday, with Nifty 50 closing at 24,056 and Sensex at 77,100.47, boosted by easing crude oil prices and supportive measures for the rupee.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Top 3 Kirloskar Group stocks to watch to add to your watchlist
articles.summary: Here are a few top Kirloskar Group stocks that you can add to your watchlist based on strong ROE and three-year CAGR of net profit
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Advit Jewels IPO: Focus shifts to allotment date. Latest GMP, step-by-step guide to check status
articles.summary: Advit Jewels IPO allotment date is set for 29 June, with a subscription rate of 212.63 times. Investors can verify allotment status on Bigshare Services' portal. Shares will credit to demat accounts on 30 June, while refunds will start the same day. Listing is on 1 July.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Apple stock price slips 6%, posts worst day in over a year after MacBook, iPad price hikes
articles.summary: Apple share price ended 6.15% lower at $275.15 apiece on the Nasdaq. During the session, the stock dropped as much as 6.59% to an intraday low of $273.75 per share, posting its biggest intraday drop since April 10, 2025.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Asian stocks today: Nikkei, Kospi fall up to 8% amid tech stocks selloff
articles.summary: Asian equities experienced a sharp decline on June 26, with semiconductor stocks weighing heavily. The MSCI Asia Pacific Index fell 1.7%, while South Korea's Kospi dropped over 3%. Meanwhile, US equity futures pointed to weaker openings, reflecting concerns in global technology markets.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Crude oil prices fall as shipments through Strait of Hormuz resume; Brent oil below $75 a barrel
articles.summary: Crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz increased this week to their highest level since the US-Iran war began in February after a ceasefire deal reopened the waterway, data showed.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Stock market holiday: BSE, NSE closed for trading today for Muharram; Commodities market to open for half day
articles.summary: Stock market holiday: According to the NSE and BSE holiday calendars, the Equity Segment, Equity Derivatives Segment, Currency Derivatives Segment, NDS-RST and Tri-Party Repo segments will remain closed for trading on Friday.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy for short term: Ajit Mishra of Religare Broking recommends 3 shares for next 1-2 weeks. Do you own any?
articles.summary: Stocks to buy for short term: Ajit Mishra of Religare Broking identifies three stocks poised for significant short-term gains, amid market caution driven by monsoon concerns.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: SpaceX effect: Indian millionaires and family offices hunt for the next big US frontier-tech payoff
articles.summary: Wealthy Indians are increasingly looking to invest in US frontier-tech and AI companies following the successful SpaceX IPO. With significant potential returns, they are accessing these opportunities to diversify globally amid muted local market returns.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: El Niño risk looms: How to monsoon-proof your portfolio?
articles.summary: As the spectre of El Niño looms, sectors tied to agriculture and rural demand are at risk. This article explores how a deficient monsoon could impact fertiliser, tractor, and two-wheeler sales, and how to adjust your investment portfolio amid economic uncertainties.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold reclaims $4,000/oz; silver rebounds by $1/oz as US dollar softens
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices showed signs of recovery on June 25, lifting gold futures by $44. Despite this, gold remains down 8% in June, while silver fell to a seven-month low. US inflation impacts are leading to cautious optimism among investors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: SpaceX credit derivatives start to trade after debut bond sale
articles.summary: Major Wall Street bond dealers are making markets on swaps tied to SpaceX after the rocket, satellite, and AI conglomerate raised $25 billion in its bond sale on Tuesday, sources said.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Advit Jewels IPO: Issue booked 213 times on Day 03; NII, QIB portions see huge demand, GMP signals 38% premium
articles.summary: Advit Jewels' IPO was oversubscribed 200 times, with robust participation across categories. The issue comprises 1.20 crore shares priced at ₹130-138, aiming to raise ₹165.16 crore for working capital and debt repayment. The stock may list at a premium, reflecting strong market interest.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Up over 100% since March! Multibagger small-cap stock to be in focus on Monday; here’s why
articles.summary: Standard Engineering plans to acquire a 51% stake in GScale Energy for ₹190 crore, marking its entry into AI data-centre infrastructure. The investment is part of a ₹500 crore program focusing on growth and capacity expansion in this emerging market.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI proposes opening term money market to NBFCs, companies
articles.summary: This move is aimed at deepening liquidity and strengthening the transmission of monetary policy across different interest-rate tenors, RBI said
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Micron shares jump 20% as strong AI-driven outlook lifts market value above Meta
articles.summary: Micron Technology surpassed Meta Platforms in market valuation for the first time, driven by a strong forecast. The shares rose 20% to $1.255, giving a market cap of $1.398 trillion, slightly above Meta's $1.392 trillion, but below Tesla's $1.4 trillion.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AI job apocalypse ‘unlikely,' could create new opportunities over time: Goldman Sachs report
articles.summary: A Goldman Sachs report says fears of an AI-driven jobs apocalypse are exaggerated, despite significant labour market disruption ahead. Experts expect AI to displace some workers while creating new jobs over time.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Iranian oil may return, but Indian refiners eye concessions
articles.summary: As Indian refiners await clarity in terms of payment mechanism and long-term durability of the US-Iran peace talks, they may not rush for immediate procurement of Iranian crude and are taking a more long-term approach, to ensure supplies when the sanctions are permanently waived off
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Will water shortages derail India’s AI Data Centre boom?
articles.summary: India's ambition to become a leading AI data centre hub brings unprecedented investments, but it also raises crucial questions about sustainability.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Weak core output, Russian crude imports, AI edge
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: For better outcomes, Centre nudges states on focused capex for ₹2 tn loan scheme
articles.summary: The DoE has asked states and UTs to limit their proposals under its SASCI scheme for capex to their choice of maximum five priority sectors in FY27. The move marks a shift from simply encouraging higher capex to ensuring more concentrated investments that deliver measurable development outcomes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Global giants backing India’s shipbuilding boom, says shipping secretary
articles.summary: Driven by a ₹70,000-crore policy package, domestic yards have secured huge export orders while the government backs five massive manufacturing clusters to scale up capacity, Vijay Kumar, secretary at the shipping ministry, told Mint in an exclusive interview.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Businesses embrace GST, but seek more reforms: Deloitte survey
articles.summary: The findings come at a time when GST collections continue to be robust. In May, the Centre and states together collected ₹1.94 trillion in gross GST revenue, before adjusting for refunds, up 3.2% from ₹1.88 trillion mopped up a year ago.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Why India’s consumers may stay cautious despite easing oil prices
articles.summary: Falling crude oil prices offer hope, but delayed rains, food inflation and higher living costs continue to weaken consumer confidence.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Millets, check dams and emergency plans: How India is preparing for dry days
articles.summary: Low monsoon rainfall is significant, as the season accounts for about 70% of India's annual rainfall. The rural economy, which relies heavily on agriculture, remains an important driver of economic growth.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: RBI allows banks to give loans for FCNR deposits
articles.summary: On 5 June, RBI said it will absorb the foreign exchange hedging cost on fresh  CNR (B), deposits, creating an unusual opportunity for overseas Indians
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Moving decisively towards finalizing trade deal with India, says US Ambassador Gor
articles.summary: India and the US are moving closer to finalizing a bilateral trade agreement, with commerce minister Piyush Goyal and USTR Jamieson Greer holding talks in New Delhi. Negotiations focus on tariffs, market access and digital trade ahead of a key US tariff deadline in July.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India's private sector output moderates further in June
articles.summary: June saw slower growth in new orders, international sales, employment and business activity.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will Singapore remain India's export star even after Iran war ends?
articles.summary: West Asian geopolitical tensions have driven a surge in India’s exports to Singapore, utilizing its robust transshipment infrastructure to diversify from traditional hubs. Will this continue?
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt plans subsidized tomato sales to cool price surge
articles.summary: In several local retail markets, tomatoes are currently being sold at ₹60-70 per kg. Retail tomato prices have jumped in India due to delays in arrivals and weather-related disruptions in producing regions.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: US trade chief to visit India for interim deal discussions
articles.summary: The visit comes amid efforts by India and the US to deepen economic ties and address market access issues through a comprehensive trade pact.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI governor asks banks to treat MSMEs as long-term partners
articles.summary: The RBI governor asked financial institutions to make greater use of India's digital public infrastructure
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI flags risks if West Asia peace deal fails, says India remains strong
articles.summary: RBI's assessment comes amid easing tensions between the US and Iran, which has helped moderate concerns around energy supply disruptions and global trade routes
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India's core sector growth hits seven-month low as Iran war bites
articles.summary: Given the tepid core sector output in May 2026, India's IIP growth is likely to weaken to 2-3% in the month from 4.9% in April 2026, according to an analyst.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gen Z is out-earning millennials, shows data; but has inflation already closed the gap?
articles.summary: New research shows Gen Z workers are out-earning millennials at the same age — but with soaring rents in the UK, US, and India, higher wages may not mean better lives. Here's what the data really shows.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: From village adulteration to organized food crime: The evolution of India’s fake milk industry
articles.summary: Evidence is emerging that in some northern states and Gujarat people are being sold fake milk that looks and tastes like the real thing. This deep dive looks at the growing sophistry in adulterating milk and how consumers can protect themselves.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The benefits and risks of RBI’s policy on foreign currency NRI deposits
articles.summary: To shore up the rupee, the government is looking at NRIs to make foreign currency deposits in Indian banks. This has implications for India’s external debt management.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India, Iran look to soon resume talks to get Chabahar Port plan back on track
articles.summary: India and Iran may resume discussions on restoring normal operations at the strategic Chabahar Port and reviving plans to expand the facility, as geopolitical conditions in the region show signs of stabilising, according to two people aware of the matter.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Young Punjab kids ‘rich for 4 yrs after selling their lands… then broke’: World Bank's Ajay Banga on urban agri economy
articles.summary: World Bank President Ajay Banga pointed out challenges in urban and agricultural economies, particularly in Punjab, where youth selling farmland quickly spend their earnings, leading to financial instability.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Views, virality and CJP protest: How India's creator economy found a new stage at Jantar Mantar
articles.summary: A May 2025 report, ‘From Content to Commerce: Mapping India’s Creator Economy’ by BCG, said that with over 2–2.5 million monetised content creators influencing more than $350–400 billion in consumer spending, the ecosystem is projected to drive $1 trillion+ in creator-influenced consumption by 2030.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: State debt concerns, WPI inflation, NSE’s IPO debut
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BEARISH
